questions: What exact acceptance commands should count for `tsc --noEmit clean for video-editor tree` and `all video-editor tests pass` in CI? I assumed a scoped `vitest` run over `src/tools/video-editor/**` plus a TypeScript command that only blocks on video-editor errors.

For `imageClipSnapshot`, do you want restore timing stored as absolute clip bounds (`at`/`end`) or as group-relative offsets/durations? The plan assumes adding explicit timing data is allowed, but that choice changes the type shape and tests.

If any saved fixtures outside `repairConfig` tests still contain projection-shaped `pinnedShotGroups`, should they be migrated in place during this refactor, or is load-time repair the only backward-compat path we need to keep?

success_criteria.criterion: `rowsToConfig()` commits preserve grouped clip adjacency across repeated interior-boundary resizes, with regression coverage proving no gap/overlap drift and no boundary jump into the neighboring clip.
`repairConfig()` converts legacy projection-shaped `pinnedShotGroups` into soft-tag groups with `clipIds` only, and migration coverage passes.

Grouped cross-track moves, final-video switching, image restore, and shot drops commit through soft-tag row/meta/clip-order/group overrides rather than legacy shim-only updates, and the affected hook tests pass.
The accepted video-editor TypeScript check passes with no in-scope errors.
The accepted video-editor Vitest sweep passes.
`PinnedShotGroup.start`, `PinnedShotGroup.children`, and the five deprecated shim exports are physically deleted with no remaining imports/usages in `src/tools/video-editor`.
No diagnostic `console.log` calls remain for `[handleResizePointerDown]`, `[outer-edge lookup]`, `[GroupEdgeResize]`, `[startGroupEdgeResize]`, `[useClipDrag]`, or `[Marquee]`.
Shot-group-related files end with a net lower line count after compatibility code is removed.
Manual smoke of the reported symptoms and the listed end-to-end flows confirms boundary releases are stable and preview frames clear correctly after release.

assumptions: The existing `translateGroupMembers()` helper in `useTimelineTrackManagement.ts` is the intended replacement for the three `moveGroup()` callsites, and extending it is preferable to adding new state flow.
A shared `4`-decimal canonical rounding policy is acceptable for video-editor commits and is sufficient to stop the interior-boundary drift without reworking `applyEdit()` or `materializeData()`.
`PinnedShotImageClipSnapshot` may gain explicit timing data because it is editor-owned pinned-group metadata, not an external public contract.
Unrelated repo-wide TypeScript or test failures remain out of scope; acceptance is limited to regressions introduced in `src/tools/video-editor`.
No production code outside `src/tools/video-editor` depends on the five deprecated shim exports or on `PinnedShotGroup.start` / `PinnedShotGroup.children`.
